payload.title: Spike: validate the offline-first cloud bet before heavy build
payload.description:
OPEN QUESTION: Will people actually pay for a hosted Scope whose differentiator is deterministic offline merge — or is that just a nice engineering story?

Why first: this is the cheapest, highest-leverage spike. If the answer is 'shrug', the whole hosted idea (Path A OR B) needs rethinking before any infra work.

Questions to resolve:
- Who is the buyer? Solo dev / small team who already syncs Scope over git, vs a team that wants 'Linear but cheap'? These want different products.
- What is the concrete pitch that gets a yes? e.g. 'a cloud node that syncs your boards to your phone without the git remote dance.'
- What would they pay (per seat? per workspace? flat?) and what is the alternative they use today?
- Is offline merge a top-3 reason to choose us, or a footnote?

How to investigate (no code): talk to 5-10 existing local Scope users. Frame it as 'would you pay for X', not 'is X cool'.

Deliverable: comment on this ticket with findings + a go/no-go recommendation. If go, refine the epic description and spawn the real phase-1 stories. If no-go, write a new epic for whatever the validated direction is.
